title: ERRO[0000] Failed to created default CNI network: error creating CNI configuration directory: mkdir /var/home/tester/.config/cni: permission denied
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug



**Description**

Running
`podman container list`
gives the error message
_ERRO[0000] Failed to created default CNI network: error creating CNI configuration directory: mkdir /var/home/tester/.config/cni: permission denied_ 

Podman version is 3.4.4.

The Fedora CoreOS version is the next stream as of today (17 Feb 2022): _fedora-coreos-35.20220213.1.0-qemu.x86_64.qcow2_



<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. On a Fedora 35 computer create the file ~/file.butane with the content (but replace the SSH public key with your SSH public key)

```
variant: fcos
version: 1.4.0
passwd:
  users:
    - name: tester
      ssh_authorized_keys:
        - ssh-ed25519 AAAAC3NzaC1lZDI1NTE5AAAAIHJ024rm4E8ygCe7m51NZEdM/WZ9a8vTLvzaSZOwikP3 test33@laptop
systemd:
  units:
    - name: serial-getty@ttyS0.service
      dropins:
      - name: autologin-core.conf
        contents: |
          [Service]
          # Override Execstart in main unit
          ExecStart=
          # Add new Execstart with `-` prefix to ignore failure`
          ExecStart=-/usr/sbin/agetty --autologin core --noclear %I $TERM
storage:
  files:
    - path: /etc/hostname
      mode: 0644
      contents:
        inline: |
          tutorial
    - path: /home/tester/startup.bash
      mode: 0644
      contents:
        inline: |
          #!/bin/bash
          cd
          git clone https://github.com/eriksjolund/mariadb-podman-socket-activation.git
          mkdir -p ~/.config/systemd/user
          cp -r mariadb-podman-socket-activation/mariadb*@* ~/.config/systemd/user
          systemctl --user daemon-reload
          systemctl --user start mariadb-tcp@demo.socket
          touch /home/tester/finished.trigger
    - path: /home/tester/.config/systemd/user/run-startup-script.service
      mode: 0644
      contents:
        inline: |
          [Unit]
          Description=Some text
          Wants=network-online.target
          After=network-online.target
          Before=zincati.service
          [Service]
          Type=oneshot
          RemainAfterExit=yes
          ExecStart=bash /home/tester/startup.bash
          [Install]
          WantedBy=multi-user.target
    - path: /var/lib/systemd/linger/tester
      mode: 0644
  directories:
    - path: /home/tester/.config/systemd/user/default.target.wants
      mode: 0755
      user:
        name: tester
      group:
        name: tester
  links:
    - path: /home/tester/.config/systemd/user/default.target.wants/run-startup-script.service
      user:
        name: tester
      group:
        name: tester
      target: /home/tester/.config/systemd/user/run-startup-script.service
      hard: false
```


Run these commands
```
sudo dnf install -y coreos-installer butane
butane --pretty --strict ~/file.butane --output /tmp/file.ign
mkdir -p ~/.local/share/libvirt/images/
STREAM=next
coreos-installer download -s "${STREAM}" -p qemu -f qcow2.xz --decompress -C ~/.local/share/libvirt/images/
cd ~/.local/share/libvirt/images
qemu-kvm -m 2048 -cpu host -nographic -snapshot -drive if=virtio,file=fedora-coreos-35.20220213.1.0-qemu.x86_64.qcow2 -fw_cfg name=opt/com.coreos/config,file=/tmp/file.ign  -nic user,model=2
```

3. Log in to the VM
```
ssh -p 2222 -o NoHostAuthenticationForLocalhost=true tester@127.0.0.1
```

4. Wait for the file _/home/tester/finished.trigger_ being created. (I'm not sure if this step is required)

5. Run `podman container list`

```
[tester@tutorial ~]$ podman container list
ERRO[0000] Failed to created default CNI network: error creating CNI configuration directory: mkdir /var/home/tester/.config/cni: permission denied 
CONTAINER ID  IMAGE       COMMAND     CREATED     STATUS      PORTS       NAMES
[tester@tutorial ~]$ echo $?
0
[tester@tutorial ~]$ 
```








**Describe the results you received:**
'


An error message is displayed
```
ERRO[0000] Failed to created default CNI network: error creating CNI configuration directory: mkdir /var/home/tester/.config/cni: permission denied 
```
**Describe the results you expected:**

No error message being displayed


**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:      3.4.4
API Version:  3.4.4
Go Version:   go1.16.8
Built:        Wed Dec  8 21:45:07 2021
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```
host:
  arch: amd64
  buildahVersion: 1.23.1
  cgroupControllers:
  - memory
  - pids
  cgroupManager: systemd
  cgroupVersion: v2
  conmon:
    package: conmon-2.1.0-2.fc35.x86_64
    path: /usr/bin/conmon
    version: 'conmon version 2.1.0, commit: '
  cpus: 1
  distribution:
    distribution: fedora
    variant: coreos
    version: "35"
  eventLogger: journald
  hostname: tutorial
  idMappings:
    gidmap:
    - container_id: 0
      host_id: 1001
      size: 1
    - container_id: 1
      host_id: 165536
      size: 65536
    uidmap:
    - container_id: 0
      host_id: 1001
      size: 1
    - container_id: 1
      host_id: 165536
      size: 65536
  kernel: 5.15.18-200.fc35.x86_64
  linkmode: dynamic
  logDriver: journald
  memFree: 1611972608
  memTotal: 2061385728
  ociRuntime:
    name: crun
    package: crun-1.4.2-1.fc35.x86_64
    path: /usr/bin/crun
    version: |-
      crun version 1.4.2
      commit: f6fbc8f840df1a414f31a60953ae514fa497c748
      spec: 1.0.0
      +SYSTEMD +SELINUX +APPARMOR +CAP +SECCOMP +EBPF +CRIU +YAJL
  os: linux
  remoteSocket:
    path: /run/user/1001/podman/podman.sock
  security:
    apparmorEnabled: false
    capabilities: CAP_CHOWN,CAP_DAC_OVERRIDE,CAP_FOWNER,CAP_FSETID,CAP_KILL,CAP_NET_BIND_SERVICE,CAP_SETFCAP,CAP_SETGID,CAP_SETPCAP,CAP_SETUID,CAP_SYS_CHROOT
    rootless: true
    seccompEnabled: true
    seccompProfilePath: /usr/share/containers/seccomp.json
    selinuxEnabled: true
  serviceIsRemote: false
  slirp4netns:
    executable: /usr/bin/slirp4netns
    package: slirp4netns-1.1.12-2.fc35.x86_64
    version: |-
      slirp4netns version 1.1.12
      commit: 7a104a101aa3278a2152351a082a6df71f57c9a3
      libslirp: 4.6.1
      SLIRP_CONFIG_VERSION_MAX: 3
      libseccomp: 2.5.3
  swapFree: 0
  swapTotal: 0
  uptime: 5m 18.08s
plugins:
  log:
  - k8s-file
  - none
  - journald
  network:
  - bridge
  - macvlan
  volume:
  - local
registries:
  search:
  - registry.fedoraproject.org
  - registry.access.redhat.com
  - docker.io
  - quay.io
store:
  configFile: /var/home/tester/.config/containers/storage.conf
  containerStore:
    number: 0
    paused: 0
    running: 0
    stopped: 0
  graphDriverName: overlay
  graphOptions: {}
  graphRoot: /var/home/tester/.local/share/containers/storage
  graphStatus:
    Backing Filesystem: xfs
    Native Overlay Diff: "true"
    Supports d_type: "true"
    Using metacopy: "false"
  imageStore:
    number: 0
  runRoot: /run/user/1001/containers
  volumePath: /var/home/tester/.local/share/containers/storage/volumes
version:
  APIVersion: 3.4.4
  Built: 1638999907
  BuiltTime: Wed Dec  8 21:45:07 2021
  GitCommit: ""
  GoVersion: go1.16.8
  OsArch: linux/amd64
  Version: 3.4.4

```

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

```
podman-3.4.4-1.fc35.x86_64
```


**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

comments.body: What are the permissions of `/var/home/tester/.config`? Podman needs to write the default network config into `~/.config/cni/net.d`

Oops, I was running a script as root that created the directory _/home/tester/.config_
```
[tester@tutorial ~]$ ls -ld .config
drwxr-xr-x. 3 root root 21 Feb 17 21:20 .config
[tester@tutorial ~]$ ls -l .config
total 0
drwxr-xr-x. 3 root root 18 Feb 17 21:20 systemd
[tester@tutorial ~]$ ls
finished.trigger  mariadb-podman-socket-activation  startup.bash
[tester@tutorial ~]$ podman container list
ERRO[0000] Failed to created default CNI network: error creating CNI configuration directory: mkdir /var/home/tester/.config/cni: permission denied 
CONTAINER ID  IMAGE       COMMAND     CREATED     STATUS      PORTS       NAMES
[tester@tutorial ~]$ ls .config
systemd
[tester@tutorial ~]$ podman container list
CONTAINER ID  IMAGE       COMMAND     CREATED     STATUS      PORTS       NAMES
[tester@tutorial ~]$ 
```
Sorry for the noise

There is currently a bug in the Fedora CoreOS documentation regarding [how to create a systemd user service](https://docs.fedoraproject.org/en-US/fedora-coreos/tutorial-user-systemd-unit-on-boot/) in a Butane file which causes _~/.config_ to be owned by root.

I opened a GitHub issue regarding this
https://github.com/coreos/fedora-coreos-docs/issues/367

In case anyone else sees such an error message

`ERRO[0000] Failed to created default CNI network: error creating CNI configuration directory: mkdir /var/home/sleeper/.config/cni: permission denied`

you might have followed that documentation.
